creator: PeterJeremy@optushome.com.au
text:
Manufacturer: HP
Model: Compaq nx6125 (EJ884PA#ABG) (discontined but some details available via http://h20331.www2.hp.com/hpsub/cache/328381-0-0-225-121.html )
Chipset/Socket: ATI Technologies Inc
BIOS: 68DTT Ver. F.07
Tested FreeBSD Versions: 6.1-RELEASE thru 6.2-PRERELEASE, 7.0-CURRENT

Broadcom WLAN works via NDIS (not in following dmesg):
ndis0: <Broadcom 802.11g Network Adapter> mem 0xd0010000-0xd0011fff irq 22 at device 2.0 on pci2
ndis0: NDIS API version: 5.1

Firewire probes but untested (and not in following dmesg):
fwohci0: <1394 Open Host Controller Interface> mem 0xd0013000-0xd00137ff,0xd0014000-0xd0017fff irq 21 at device 4.2 on pci2
fwohci0: OHCI version 1.10 (ROM=0)
fwohci0: No. of Isochronous channels is 4.
fwohci0: EUI64 00:02:3f:99:29:99:44:0a
fwohci0: Phy 1394a available S400, 2 ports.
fwohci0: Link S400, max_rec 2048 bytes.
firewire0: <IEEE1394(FireWire) bus> on fwohci0
fwe0: <Ethernet over FireWire> on firewire0
if_fwe0: Fake Ethernet address: 02:02:3f:99:44:0a
fwe0: Ethernet address: 02:02:3f:99:44:0a
fwe0: if_start running deferred for Giant
fwohci0: Initiate bus reset
fwohci0: node_id=0xc800ffc0, gen=1, CYCLEMASTER mode
firewire0: 1 nodes, maxhop <= 0, cable IRM = 0 (me)
firewire0: bus manager 0 (me)

Bluetooth works:
ubt0: Broadcom HP integrated Bluetooth module, rev 1.10/0.17, addr 2
ubt0: Interface 0 endpoints: interrupt=0x81, bulk-in=0x82, bulk-out=0x2
ubt0: Interface 1 (alt.config 4) endpoints: isoc-in=0x83, isoc-out=0x3; wMaxPacketSize=64; nframes=5, buffer size=320

Unsupported devices: WinModem, Flash card-reader, Fingerprint scanner:
none1@pci0:20:6:        class=0x070300 card=0x308b103c chip=0x43781002 rev=0x02 hdr=0x00
    vendor   = 'ATI Technologies Inc'
    class    = simple comms
    subclass = generic modem
none5@pci2:4:3: class=0x018000 card=0x308b103c chip=0x8033104c rev=0x00 hdr=0x00
    vendor   = 'Texas Instruments (TI)'
    device   = 'PCIxx21 Integrated FlashMedia Controller'
    class    = mass storage
Controller /dev/usb1:
addr 1: full speed, self powered, config 1, OHCI root hub(0x0000), ATI(0x0000), rev 1.00
 port 1 addr 2: full speed, power 100 mA, config 1, Fingerprint Sensor(0x2580), vendor 0x08ff(0x08ff), rev 6.21

X.org does not have accelerated support for the ATI Radeon XPRESS 200M 5955.  Switching between X and VTYs occasionally produces a corrupt display but closing and opening the lid almost always fixes this.

DVD does not appear to work in DMA mode.

powerd causes random hard lockups (there is a problem with speed changing under load)

Suspend doesn't work (either spins or panics)
